topic: flor yeast and biological ageing in sherry
trigger_keywords: rol juega la flor en la crianza biológica del Jerez
protege al vino del oxígeno y desarrolla sabores únicos

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Flor yeast (a film of Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains) forms on the surface of the wine in partially filled barrels

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The flor layer provides oxygen protection by excluding oxygen from the wine surface; the yeast metabolises ethanol and glycerol, producing acetaldehyde, and undergoes autolysis adding amino acids and breadlike compounds

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine develops biological ageing character: almond, bread dough, and yeasty notes, low colour, low tannin, protected from oxidation despite barrel ageing

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Flor yeast provides oxygen protection, producing biological ageing character: almond and bread dough flavours develop while oxidation is prevented by the flor layer.

sat_relevance: Use when tasting or discussing Fino or Manzanilla sherry style. Biological ageing character (almond, bread, low colour) is a product of flor protection, distinct from oxidative ageing aromas.
